subject: A respectful request: commit to scrapping VNI West and the WRL
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

I'm writing respectfully as one of many Victorians deeply worried about the VNI West and Western Renewables Link transmission projects.

These projects would carve through some of our best farmland, force easements onto properties families have worked for generations, and put local food production at risk. The communities in their path do not feel they have been listened to.

You have the chance to stand with farming families where the Government would not. I'm asking you to commit to scrapping both projects and to back smarter alternatives that don't cost us our best agricultural land.

Projects like these should never be forced over the top of the people who feed this state without genuine consultation.

A clear commitment from the Coalition would mean a great deal to regional Victoria. Thank you for your time.

Kind regards,

subject: Our best farmland only gets used once
body:
Dear Jess Wilson, Danny O'Brien and David Davis,

Victoria's best farmland only gets used once. Once transmission easements cut across it, the paddocks that grow our food are changed for good.

That is why I'm asking you, respectfully, to commit the Coalition to scrapping VNI West and the Western Renewables Link. Both projects route straight through prime agricultural country and the families who work it.

Farmers in the path of these lines aren't asking for special treatment. They're asking for the chance to keep producing food for Victorian families on land their parents and grandparents worked.

There are far better options than building over the top of our most productive country. Please put those alternatives on the table and rule these two projects out.

Regional Victoria is hoping you'll be the ones who listen. Thank you for considering this.

Yours sincerely,

subject: Behind every kilometre of these routes is a family
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

Behind every kilometre of the proposed VNI West and Western Renewables Link routes is a family. Many have been on their land for three or four generations. They've been told towers are coming whether they like it or not.

I'm writing to ask, politely but urgently, that the Coalition commit to scrapping both projects.

Nobody in these communities is against progress. What they're against is being treated as an obstacle rather than as people, and watching their life's work carved up when better options exist.

This is a genuine chance for the Liberals and Nationals to show regional Victoria that someone in Spring Street is listening. I hope you'll take it.

Thank you for reading this, and for everything you do for our regions.

Kind regards,

subject: VNI West and the WRL are the test of what the Liberals stand for
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

The Liberal Party has always told us it stands for property rights and for the regions. VNI West and the Western Renewables Link are the test of that.

These projects would push easements onto working farms without the genuine consent of the people who own and work that land. Families would carry the cost of infrastructure planned in city offices, far from the paddocks it crosses.

I'm asking you, respectfully, to commit to scrapping both projects and championing alternatives that don't take our best farmland with them.

If the Coalition makes that commitment, you'll have tens of thousands of regional Victorians standing behind you. We'd much rather write to thank you than to fight.

Thank you for your consideration.

Yours sincerely,

subject: There's still time to do the right thing
body:
Dear Jess Wilson, Danny O'Brien and David Davis,

There's still time to do the right thing on VNI West and the Western Renewables Link, and I'm writing to ask you to take it.

These transmission projects would cut through prime farmland and change some of Victoria's most productive country forever. The families in their path have raised their concerns for years and feel they've been ignored at every step.

You can be the leaders who finally listen. I'm respectfully asking the Coalition to commit to scrapping both projects and to stand up for planning that respects regional communities instead of steamrolling them.

An earnest request from rural Victoria: please stand with farming families. It would not be forgotten.

With thanks and respect,

subject: The people in the path of these lines deserve better
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

I'm writing about VNI West and the Western Renewables Link, and about the way the people in their path have been treated.

Farming families along these routes describe consultation that felt like a formality, decisions made before they were asked, and pressure to accept easements across land they've spent lifetimes improving. Whatever your view on transmission, that is not how Victorians should be treated.

I'm asking the Coalition to draw a line: commit to scrapping both projects and to a planning process that starts with the communities affected, not with a route already drawn on a map.

You have a real opportunity to restore some trust in regional Victoria. I hope you'll take it, and I thank you for your time.

Kind regards,

subject: Two badly planned projects. Please rule them out.
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

I want to be clear about what I'm asking: please stand in the way of two badly planned projects.

VNI West and the Western Renewables Link route through some of the best farmland in the state, while alternatives, from different corridors to smarter network planning, have never been seriously put to affected communities.

Please commit the Coalition to scrapping both projects as they stand and to an honest assessment of the alternatives.

Regional Victorians are tired of having projects like these done to them. A commitment from you would show that someone in Spring Street is finally listening.

Thank you sincerely for your consideration.

Yours faithfully,

subject: Regional Victoria is watching what you do next
body:
Dear Jess Wilson, Danny O'Brien and David Davis,

Regional Victoria is watching what happens next with VNI West and the Western Renewables Link, and so am I.

For farming families along these routes, this isn't an abstract infrastructure debate. It's their homes, their soil and their livelihoods. They have asked, over and over, for someone in Parliament to take their side.

I'm respectfully asking the Coalition to be the ones who do: commit to scrapping both projects and to protecting prime farmland in whatever comes next.

This is the kind of decision our communities remember for a long time. I truly hope we'll be remembering it as the moment you stood with us.

Thank you for your time and your service.

Kind regards,

subject: A short, polite request on VNI West and the WRL
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

I'll keep this short. Please commit the Coalition to scrapping VNI West and the Western Renewables Link.

These projects take prime farmland from families who have worked it for generations, and the communities affected don't believe they've been listened to. Victoria should never build over the top of the people who grow our food.

This is a polite request from an ordinary Victorian who cares about our regions: please stand with farming families and rule these projects out.

Thank you for reading, and for considering it.

Kind regards,

subject: In the country, you show up for your neighbours
body:
Dear Ms Wilson, Mr O'Brien and Mr Davis,

In the country, when a neighbour is in trouble, you show up. Right now the families in the path of VNI West and the Western Renewables Link are in trouble, and tens of thousands of us are showing up for them.

I'm one of them, and I'm asking you respectfully: commit to scrapping both projects.

These lines would carve up productive farmland, put towers over properties held for generations, and leave communities feeling like their consent never mattered. Our neighbours deserve better, and Victoria has better options.

The Coalition can be the voice these communities have been waiting for. Please be that voice.

With respect and thanks,
